output.text:
To expand and simplify the given expression $x\left(x(1+x)+2x\right)-3(x^2-x+2)$, we follow these steps: ки
1. Distribute $x$ inside the first set of parentheses:
\begin{align*}
x\left(x(1+x)+2x\right)-3(x^2-x+2) &= x(x \cdot 1 + x \cdot x + 2x) - 3(x^2 - x + 2) \\
&= x(x + x^2 + 2x) - 3(x^2 - x + 2) \\
&= x^2 + x^3 + 2x^2 - 3(x^2 - x + 2) \\
\end{align*} ки
2. Distribute $-3$ to each term inside the parentheses:
\begin{align*}
x^2 + x^3 + 2x^2 - 3(x^2 - x + 2) &= x^2 + x^3 + 2x^2 - 3x^2 + 3x - 6 \\
\end{align*} ки
3. Combine like terms:
\begin{align*}
x^2 + x^3 + 2x^2 - 3x^2 + 3x - 6 &= x^3 + (x^2 + 2x^2 - 3x^2) + 3x - 6 \\
&= x^3 - x^2 + 3x - 6
\end{align*} ки
Therefore, the fully expanded and simplified expression is $\boxed{x^3 - x^2 + 3x - 6}$. ки
